observations.recommendation:
Management to require 
all concerned         
officials and         
employees to liquidate
and/or settle their   
cash advances         
immediately. Also,    
send follow-up and/or 
demand letters        
requiring the         
personnel concerned to
liquidate, otherwise,  
impose the withholding
of salaries and other  
emoluments due to the  
concerned officials   
and employees until   
full                  
liquidation/settlement
of their unliquidated  
cash advances. For    
those who are no     
longer connected with
the Municipality,     
demand letters should 
be sent again to their 
present addresses or  
stations.

Management to direct  
the Municipal Market  
Committee to take     
action and resolve the
above violations by   
imposing penalties or 
declaring these units
vacant and then award 
through public        
bidding, whichever is 
deemed appropriate.   
Also, require the     
Committee to conduct  
public bidding for    
vacant units and to   
allow only those      
individuals/groups who
are declared winners  
thereat to occupy     
these units. In case  
some provisions are   
found to be impractical
or deemed not doable  
on the part of the    
leaseholders, the     
Municipal Market      
Committee may request
from the Sangguniang  
Bayan the amendments
of these provisions,   
provided however,     
that such amendments
would still protect   
the interest of the   
LGU.

Management to expedite
the completion of GAD 
Database and updated  
CMBS to serve as basis
for gender-responsive 
planning, programming 
and policy formulation.

Management to direct: 
The Municipal         
Treasurer to:         
Establish the balances
of the 15% share of   
the OBO and the 5%   
share of the DPWH from
CY 2019 and earlier   
years, and transfer    
thereafter the share  
of the OBO to its TF  
account and remit the 
share of the DPWH to  
the BTr;              
Prepare a separate    
Report of Collections 
for subsequent shares
of the OBO and deposit
directly to its TF     
account. Likewise, the
share of the DPWH is  
to be directly        
remitted to the BTr;  
Show in the Official  
Receipt the breakdown
of the shares of the   
LGU, OBO and DPWH from
the collections of    
building permit fees  
and other charges.    
The Municipal         
Accountant to:        
Make the necessary   
adjustments to correct
the erroneous         
recording of the     
shares of the OBO and
DPWH and the transfer
of the 15% share of   
the OBO to TF and the
remittance of shares  
of the DWPH, as      
follows:             
Recognize only 80% of
the collections from  
building permit fees  
and other charges as  
Permit Fees in the GF 
books; and            
Account the 15% share
of the OBO in the TF   
books as Trust        
Liabilities and the 5%
share of the DPWH as 
Due to NGAs in the    
General Fund. A      
separate Subsidiary  
Ledger is to be      
maintained for this  
purpose/fund.

Management to         
immediately formulate
a specific safety plan
to cover all aspects  
of fire safety and to  
correct the above     
deficiencies to avoid
casualties and        
incurring fines and  
penalties for        
non-compliance        
thereof.

Management to follow   
the rules and         
regulations for       
disposal of           
unserviceable         
properties and the    
dropping thereafter   
in the books in       
accordance with COA   
Circular mentioned    
above.

Follow the above       
guidelines on the    
proper                
disposition/closure   
thereof, whichever is 
applicable, per COA   
Circular No. 97-001,
dated February 5,    
1997. Determine      
whether or not these 
accounts could still  
be continued and     
used. Otherwise,     
remittance of these   
account balances to  
the National Treasury
is, likewise,         
recommended.

7. We recommended that management direct  the  Municipal  Treasurer  to
       immediately submit the corresponding RCDs to the Accounting Unit and
       to prepare timely the RCDs for subsequent collections to ensure that
       receipts of remittances made by other Funds are  recognized  on  the
       same period to which they relate  in  order  to  avoid  unreconciled
       reciprocal accounts balances.


    8. Likewise, require the Municipal Accountant  to  make  the  necessary
       adjustments to account the collection from the equipment  rental  in
       the GF books of accounts as follows:


       Due from Other Funds             P19,500
            Prior Period Adjustment                P19,500


    9. Moreover, direct the Municipal Accountant to determine the cause  of
       the unreconciled  difference  of  P124,401  for  the  municipal  and
       provincial shares on Special Education Taxes and make the  necessary
       adjustments thereto.

4. We recommended that management require the Municipal  Treasurer  and
       Municipal Accountant to come up with a strategy  on  how  to  obtain
       documents/data on the unresolved reconciling  items  especially  for
       misplaced/lost disbursement vouchers and  undetermined  credit/debit
       memos.


    5. Moreover, demand from the concerned Barangay  Treasurers  to  submit
       the corresponding Summary of Collections and  Remittances  of  these
       unreported deposits to the Office of the Municipal Treasurer and  to
       submit thereafter to the Accounting Unit for recording in the  books
       of accounts.


    6.  Further,  direct  the  Municipal  Accountant  to  determine   those
       reconciling items shown  in  Appendix  D  as  to  its  validity  and
       immediately recognize in the books of accounts the valid reconciling
       items.

6. We recommended that management require the MDRRMO, MSWDO and MHO  to
       prepare and submit to the Accounting Unit the SSMI and RIS with  the
       List  of  Recipients  with  their  corresponding  signature   and/or
       photocopies of logbooks which acknowledged the receipts of the above
       items by the recipients. Also, require  these  Offices  to  maintain
       stock cards for each item in order to determine the balance for each
       item and the need to re-order before the items are fully consumed.


    7. For the food supplies issued by  the  MSWDO,  direct  the  MSWDO  to
       present proof of submission of documents to the Accounting Unit  for
       the distributed food supplies and after which, the  Accounting  Unit
       to locate/retrieve said documents.

6. We recommended that management direct the  Municipal  Accountant  to
       trace those transactions which caused the abnormal negative balances
       and effect the necessary  adjustments  in  order  to  present  these
       accounts fairly in the financial statements.

7. We recommended that management require the Municipal  Accountant  to
       recognize depreciation expenses and  the  corresponding  accumulated
       depreciation of the above depreciable assets to fairly  present  the
       affected accounts in the financial statements at year end.

7. We recommended that management:

       a. Require the Municipal Engineer to identify the road lot  component
          of each municipal road, its area measurement and fair  value  with
          the coordination of the Municipal  Assessor,  and  submit  to  the
          Municipal Accountant. Also, determine the  locations  of  the  two
          municipal roads named as concreting of municipal streets 2016  and
          2017 and the corresponding details; and


       b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with  the  Municipal
          Engineer to determine those road components which are  erroneously
          recognized under  the  account  Flood  Control  System.  Make  the
          necessary adjustments to correct the erroneous recording  of  road
          components and to account road lots in the books  of  accounts  as
          part of the Road Networks. Update the two LRNLC and maintain LRNLC
          for the other 57 municipal roads.

23. We recommended that management:

       a. Direct the Inventory Committee to  prepare  a  Physical  Inventory
          Plan (PIP) and submit the same to  the  COA  Audit  Team  for  the
          subsequent physical count at least ten calendar  days  before  the
          scheduled start of inventory taking activities, together with  the
          Invitation Letter to  witness  the  entire  inventory  taking,  in
          compliance with the pertinent provisions  of  Section  5  of  said
          Circular;


       b. Require the Inventory Committee to  record/document  the  physical
          count daily using the ICF per PPE sub-major account group and will
          be the basis in the preparation of RPCPPE, List of  PPE  Found  at
          Station and List of Non-Existing/Missing PPEs;


       c. Inform the Inventory Committee to submit the RPCPPE as of December
          31, 2021 to the Audit Team on or before September 30, 2022;


       d. Direct the Property Unit to  prepare  a  List  of  PPEs  Found  at
          Station  (Annex  B  of   said   Circular)   and   List   of   Non-
          Existing/Missing PPEs (Annex C of said Circular)  during  physical
          inventory and submit  these  lists  to  the  Accounting  Unit  for
          recording in the books of accounts of the  LGU  to  establish  the
          existence and proper accountability of the PPE accounts; and


       e. Ensure that the  guidelines  and  procedures  in  the  conduct  of
          physical count of PPE as provided in  COA  Circular  2020-006  are
          strictly observed to facilitate  the  one-time  cleansing  of  PPE
          account balances.

7. We recommended that management  make  initiatives  to  at  least  50
       percent of the unremitted collections are settled  within  CY  2022.
       Also, consider the filing of appropriate administrative and criminal
       charges against those concerned accountable officers, if they  still
       fail to abide with the agreement.

6. We recommended that management direct  the  Municipal  Treasurer  to
       prepare weekly schedule of remittances in case daily  remittance  is
       not  suitable  under  the  normal  circumstances  to   ensure   that
       collections are remitted one  to  two  times  weekly  to  avoid  its
       accumulation and the risk  of  possible  loss  thru  robbery  and/or
       misappropriation or misuse.


    7. Also, stop the practice of remitting collections only when the  stub
       of accountable form is fully consumed/issued. Even if  the  stub  is
       yet to be fully consumed/issued, the  corresponding  collections  of
       the issued accountable forms  are  to  be  remitted  on  the  weekly
       remittance schedule recommended above.


    8. Moreover, direct the Collecting Officer  to  remit  all  collections
       intact. Accountable forms issued after previous remittance up to the
       coming scheduled remittance are to be  completely  reported  in  the
       covering Report of Collections and Deposits  and  all  corresponding
       collections are to be remitted to the Liquidating Officer  to  avoid
       lapping of collections.

7. We recommended that Management come up with a  strategy  on  how  to
       locate  these  unreported  accountable  forms.  Also,  continue  the
       withholding  of  their  salaries  until  full  compliance   thereof.
       Consider  the  filing  of  administrative  sanctions  against  these
       accountable officers for failure to render accounts.

5. We recommended that management  direct  the  Disbursing  Officer  to
       reflect those unrecorded cash advances and disbursements/payments in
       her cashbook to determine her periodic accountability,  as  detailed
       in Appendix H. Also, require her to make the  necessary  adjustments
       for   the    erroneous    recording    of    cash    advances    and
       disbursements/payments, as shown in Appendix I.


    6. Likewise, require the Disbursing Officer to post in the cashbook all
       subsequent  cash   advances   and   disbursements/payments   thereof
       correctly and completely, as required  under  Section  28  of  NGAS.
       Moreover, all  refunds  of  undisbursed  cash  advances  are  to  be
       reflected in the cashbook on the month the refunds are made in order
       to correctly show the actual undisbursed cash advances  at  the  end
       and beginning of each month.

6. We recommended that management direct the  Municipal  Accountant  to
       recognize cash advances as liquidated in the books of accounts  only
       when the corresponding Report of  Disbursements/Liquidation  Reports
       are submitted by the AO and verified by the Accounting Unit. This is
       to show the actual balance of  cash  advances  periodically  and  to
       avoid granting of additional cash  advances  to  AO  with  unsettled
       previous cash advances.

5. We recommended  that management:

       a. Evaluate the viability of  projects  which  were  not  implemented
          during  the  year  to  determine  the  cause/s   of   their   non-
          implementation. If not viable, consider the reprogramming of  said
          funds to another project; and


       b. Direct the Local Development Council and the Municipal Engineer to
          closely monitor and pursue the timely and full  implementation  of
          all the developmental projects.

10. We recommended that management require the concerned front liners/health
        workers to  submit  to  COA  Office  the  proof  of  attendance  for
        the computed excess working  days  as  shown  in  the  attached
        Appendices K and L. Also, submit the Job  Order  Contract  and  DTRs
        for September 2020 of the 1 frontliner (laborer) which found to be
        without DTR.


    11. Moreover, direct the Municipal Health Officer to include only those
        front liners/health workers who are eligible for subsequent AHDP and
        SRA and to be presented separately in order  to  determine  who  are
        eligible only for AHDP or SRA. Also,  the  no.  of  days  physically
        reporting for work are to be taken from their respective DTRs, if
        services are already rendered, to avoid excess payment of AHDP and
        SRA.


    12. Also, instruct the concerned personnel to attach  the  DTRs  of  the
        front liners/health workers in the  Disbursement  Voucher/Report  of
        Disbursements to support the payment of AHDP and  SRA  in  order  to
        ensure that the no. of days physically reported for work as declared
        is the actual no. of days these workers  reported  for  work.  Also,
        DTRs are to be properly accomplished, indicating therein the time in
        and out for night shift, on call and overtime.

6. We recommended that management avoid the charging of expenditures in
       the GAD Funds for activities not within the GAD Plan.  Also,  direct
       the GFPS to regularly monitor the GAD Funds to ensure that funds are
       being spent for what they were intended for.

7. We  recommended  that  management  expedite  the  completion  of  an
       operational GAD Database and updated CMBS  to  serve  as  basis  for
       gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation.

Management to:        
Require the MGSO with 
the coordination of   
the Municipal Engineer
to prepare the        
necessary documents to	ransfer/turn-over    
those roads not       
identified as         
municipal roads to the
the concerned agencies.
These documents will  
be the basis of the   
Municipal Accountant  
to make the necessary 
adjustments to drop   
these assets from the 
books of accounts. The
adjusting entries are 
as follows:           
                      
Government Equity     
P66,209,423.60        
Accumulated           
Depreciation-Road     
Networks              
4,048,323.37          
Road Networks         
P66,209,423.60        
Prior Period          
Adjustment            
4,048,323.37          
To recognize          
transferred/turned-ove
r barangay road       
projects.             
                      
Moreover, direct the  
Municipal Accountant  
to require the        
barangay bookkeeper to
record those road     
projects under the    
jurisdiction of the   
Barangays in their    
respective books of   
accounts.

Management to direct  
the Municipal         
Environment and       
Natural Resources     
Officer and Municipal 
Engineer not to charge
expenditures related  
to the operation of   
solid waste management
and other general     
services expenses     
against the 20 percent
DF. Also, regularly   
recurring             
administrative         
expenses included in  
the PPAs be provided   
in their respective    
appropriate budget in 
the General Fund. In  
order for wages to be 
related to the        
implementation of the 
projects, the services 
of PIMF are to be     
provided in the POW   
with equivalent cost  
to be incurred.

Management to hire    
workers in accordance
with the              
implementation period 
of the project to     
avoid disparity of    
wage-period from the 
delivery date and     
rental period of heavy
equipment. Also,     
direct the Municipal  
Engineer to recommend
workers to be hired in
accordance with the   
approved POW. At no   
instances would       
workers’ designations 
be unrelated to the   
projects.

Management direct the
Technical Working     
Group to conduct post 
qualification to its  
Lowest Calculated     
Bid/Highest Rated Bid 
to ensure that the    
bidder concerned      
complies with and is  
responsive to all the 
legal requirements and
conditions specified    
in the Bidding        
Documents before      
awarding the contract,
as required under     
Section 34.1 and 34.3 
of the 2016 IRR of RA 
9184.

Management to direct   
the official to whom  
the vehicle is        
assigned to allow the 
use of vehicle and to 
be provided with fuel 
by the Fuel Custodian  
only on a duly         
accomplished and      
approved Driver’s Trip 
Ticket to ensure that  
expenses related       
thereto are charged to
its pertinent           
appropriations.

Management to direct  
the Municipal         
Accountant to prepare
and submit the Report 
on Sources and        
Utilization of DRRMF  
monthly, in accordance
with Section 5.1.5 of  
COA Circular No.     
2012-002 to ensure    
that expenditures made
from this fund could  
be properly monitored,
analyzed and evaluated
timely by our Office  
and the LDRRMC/LDC.

Management to:        
Reprogram the balances
of appropriations of  
completed projects to 
finance other priority
development projects.

Management to         
prioritize the        
creation and          
appointment of the    
remaining one         
mandatory position    
responsible for       
research and planning,
in consonance with 4.2
of JMC 2014-1.

Management to furnish  
the Auditor with the  
required copies of    
contract              
documents/purchase/let
ter/job orders and    
their supporting      
documents as well as  
notice of deliveries  
of procured items     
within the prescribed 
period as required    
under COA Circular No.
2009-00.
